title: 3-Bedroom Houses for Rent in Hough, Cleveland
metaTitle: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Hough, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Find 3 bedroom houses for rent in Hough, Cleveland (44103), near the HealthLine and University Circle. See typical rent and book a free showing.
primaryKeyword: 3 bedroom houses for rent Hough cleveland
answerBox: Three-bedroom houses in Hough, Cleveland (ZIP 44103) typically rent from about $1,050 to $1,400 a month, close to Cleveland's citywide 3-bedroom median of $1,350 reported by Zumper for July 2026. Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-friendly three-bedroom homes throughout Hough — every property accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready.

body: Hough sits on Cleveland's east side in **ZIP code 44103**, between Downtown Cleveland to the west and University Circle to the east, with Euclid Avenue forming its northern edge. Three-bedroom layouts here are common in the neighborhood's early-1900s wood-frame housing stock, which typically adds a full basement and a small back yard compared to smaller one- and two-bedroom units on the same streets — useful extra space regardless of household size.

Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-friendly three-bedroom rental homes throughout Hough. Every property we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see current three-bedroom availability in the neighborhood.

sections.h2: How much does a 3-bedroom house cost in Hough?
body: Three-bedroom homes in Hough generally rent from about $1,050 to $1,400 a month, depending on condition, size, and proximity to Euclid Avenue. That range sits close to Zumper's July 4, 2026 citywide 3-bedroom median of $1,350/month — a much tighter gap than RentCafe's July 2, 2026 figure of $2,716 for a 3-bedroom, which reflects larger, professionally-managed apartment buildings rather than the smaller single-family homes and duplexes typical of Hough.

sections.h2: What a 3-bedroom home in Hough looks like
body: Most three-bedroom rentals in Hough are single-family houses or the larger unit of a two-family duplex, built in the early-to-mid 1900s with front porches and modest side yards. Bedrooms are typically arranged on a single upper floor, with a full basement below often used for laundry and storage. Because the housing stock predates modern construction, layouts vary more than in newer buildings, so a showing is the most reliable way to confirm room sizes and closet space before applying.

items: **Typical structure:** Single-family house or duplex upper/lower unit
**Common features:** Front porch, full basement, small back yard
**Best next step:** Book a showing to confirm layout and condition

sections.h2: Getting around from a 3-bedroom home in Hough
body: Hough runs along the **Euclid Avenue HealthLine**, GCRTA's bus rapid transit route linking Public Square downtown to University Circle and East Cleveland through a dedicated center-median busway. The Red Line's University Circle station is also within reach near the neighborhood's southern edge, putting Cleveland Clinic, Case Western Reserve University, and the Cleveland Museum of Art a short transit ride away. Rockefeller Park and its Cultural Gardens line the neighborhood's southern boundary along Martin Luther King Jr. Drive, offering nearby green space.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers for 3-bedroom homes in Hough
body: Every three-bedroom home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in Hough accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. The Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) sets a separate payment standard for each bedroom size, so the maximum subsidy for a 3-bedroom voucher differs from a 2-bedroom; check CMHA's current chart directly at [cmha.net](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) rather than relying on a fixed number, since payment standards are updated periodically. For nearby three-bedroom comparisons on Cleveland's east side, see [3-bedroom homes in Glenville](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-glenville-cleveland/) or read our full [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/). If price is your bigger concern than bedroom count, our [cheap houses in Hough guide](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/cheap-houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/) breaks down lower-cost options in the same neighborhood.

sections.h2: Lease details worth checking on an older 3-bedroom in Hough
body: Because most of Hough's three-bedroom housing dates to the early 1900s, it falls under Cleveland's Codified Ordinances Chapter 365, which requires a **Lead-Safe Certification** for any residential rental unit built before January 1, 1978. The certification is good for two years and follows a visual and dust-wipe inspection by a certified risk assessor, so it's worth asking to see a current certificate before you sign for a three-bedroom home in the neighborhood.

It's also worth confirming which utility serves the specific address before move-in: electric service in Hough comes from either Cleveland Public Power or The Illuminating Company depending on location, and gas is typically supplied by Enbridge Gas Ohio. A larger three-bedroom home generally carries higher utility costs than a smaller unit, so factoring that into your monthly budget alongside rent is worthwhile.
